meta.owner: Clay White — Product Manager, Digital Tools Portfolio, VusionGroup
meta.role_note: Clay is PM of the digital-tools portfolio — accountable for owners, commitments, and outcomes across workstreams, not the default IC on every line. He is one IC among several on hands-on work (select UI).
meta.scope: Digital tools that enable VusionGroup products (TopStock Rail, Captana, ESL)
team: Clay White — PM (and IC on select UI work)
Zel — beta testing + physical inspection / Gen1 daily walks

projects.name: TopStock Companion App
projects.serves: TopStock Rail (Walmart rollout — 5 Gen1 stores, AR/TX)
projects.next_milestone: Validation live on the 100-rail test install
blockers: Eric — Validation pipeline fix (hardware-state → UI). Pending Titouan meeting 6/16; Eric's wall is Fri 6/19 midday CT.
Cristina — add/delete Sections build; ETA pending.
Supervisor RBAC — installers → Install, supervisors → Validation.
projects.notes: Real deadline is the 100-rail test install Mon 6/22 (6/19 was a proxy). Gen2 can't run on Gen1, so the 100-rail test site is the validation opportunity. Validation = supervisor-facing labor-state board (not-yet-installed → installed-not-tested → tested-not-passed → passed) that produces the digital QC record; Zel does the physical inspection. No-weekend rule: Sat/Sun 6/20–21 are parachute-only — all cross-team work done by Fri 6/19. Wed 6/17 is the go/no-go checkpoint. Demo: built but unvalidated, needs a HW/SW integration test (a working Demo also confirms rail function) — lead domino, do early week. Maintenance UI is delivered/done (Clay's part, has user feedback); remaining monitoring & alerting system is net-new backend/integration being scoped as a separate project, with a real deadline that likely tracks rail deployment rather than 7/10. Validation severity model (red=confirmed failure, orange=flagged awaiting supervisor, yellow=installed/test pending) with Run Test buttons at aisle and section level remains in place; rail_detail untouched.

changelog.entry: Tracker initialized. Install + Demo flows marked done.
Maintenance module flipped to in_progress (work started early; due 7/3 unchanged).

Validation flow severity model implemented across overview/zone/aisle pages (red=confirmed test failure, orange=flagged awaiting supervisor, yellow=installed/test pending); added on-demand Run Test buttons at aisle and section level. rail_detail untouched; due 6/19 unchanged, Validation on track.

Validation re-anchored from 6/19 to 6/22 — the 100-rail test install Mon 6/22 is the real deadline; 6/19 was a proxy. Project status → at_risk. Validation defined as the supervisor labor-state board producing the digital QC record (Zel does physical inspection). Blockers added: Eric Validation pipeline fix (hardware-state→UI; pending Titouan mtg 6/16; wall Fri 6/19 midday CT); Cristina add/delete Sections (ETA pending); Supervisor RBAC. No-weekend rule (6/20–21 parachute-only, cross-team done by Fri 6/19); Wed 6/17 go/no-go.
Demo reopened to in_progress — built but unvalidated, needs HW/SW integration test (also confirms rail function); lead domino, early week (target 6/17).

Maintenance UI delivered/done (has user feedback). Date slipped 7/3 → 7/10 due to production delays (not Clay's scope). Remaining monitoring & alerting system = net-new backend/integration, being scoped as a separate project; real deadline likely tracks rail deployment, not 7/10.

projects.name: QC Tool — Post-Install Inspection
projects.serves: TopStock Rail / Walmart first; multi-product later
supporting: Eric (backend — data pull reuses his pipeline)
projects.notes: Post-install inspection, TopStock/Walmart priority. v1 (Working, 7/15) = one UI (designed to hold images) + data pull reusing Eric's pipeline + text deficiencies + hosted + internal-only RBAC. Complete-with-images ~7/25 is an estimate, gated on the image-storage decision; images are a fast-follow staged into the same UI. Starts after the 6/22 test install. Build happens in its own project. Outcome = a reliable post-install QC record for TopStock.

changelog.entry: v1 (Working) target set 7/15 (was 7/10). Scope defined: single image-ready UI + Eric-pipeline data pull + text deficiencies + hosted + internal-only RBAC. Complete-with-images ~7/25 fast-follow, gated on image-storage decision. Starts after the 6/22 test install.

projects.name: GT Tool v2
supporting: Connor (stakeholder — recent improvements delivered for him)
projects.next_milestone: Scope decision: build v2 vs adopt/adapt existing France tool
blockers: Parked pending France lead reply (expected 6/16) — France may already run a tool in use; v2 may become adopt/adapt rather than build.
projects.notes: Parked and deprioritized — decoupled from the Sainsbury's push; lower priority (Walmart always wins). Prior 6/26 build target is on hold pending the scope decision. v1 live and filterable by time, location, camera spacing. Outcome = eliminate manual image-checking.

changelog.entry: Tracker initialized. v2 goal set for 6/26.

GT v2 parked pending France lead reply (expected 6/16) — possible adopt/adapt vs build. Decoupled from Sainsbury's; deprioritized (Walmart priority). Prior 6/26 build target on hold. Status → blocked. Outcome framed: eliminate manual image-checking.

projects.name: Site Survey Tool
projects.next_milestone: Plan + timeline drafted (planning work happens in that project; timeline TBD until plan exists)
projects.notes: Discovery phase: researching comparable market tools and documenting the current highly manual process before designing a replacement. TopStock Site Survey Documentation session held 6/11.

changelog.entry: Tracker initialized. In planning; no committed dates yet.

carry_forwards.item: Definitive answer on camera-behind-glass scope (Sainsbury's Captana pilot)
carry_forwards.resolution: Closed per Clay 6/15 — taken off Clay's plate.
carry_forwards.rule: Carries forward weekly until resolved. Cannot be deleted — only resolved with date + resolution note.

carry_forwards.resolution: Closed per Clay 6/15 — taken off Clay's plate.
carry_forwards.rule: Carries forward weekly until resolved. Cannot be deleted — only resolved with date + resolution note.

carry_forwards.item: Blink ID reliability escalation — random, non-reproducible ESL misses with no command acknowledgement, retry, or event-level traceability
carry_forwards.resolution: Closed per Clay 6/15 — taken off Clay's plate.
carry_forwards.rule: Active escalation. Carries forward weekly until resolved.

recurring_deliverables.name: Weekly leadership slide
recurring_deliverables.standard: Thaddeus directive: single slide, max 200 words, delete unused boxes, relevance over completeness

recurring_deliverables.name: Weekly recap email
recurring_deliverables.standard: Subject format: Weekly Recap | [Date] | Product Implementation. SharePoint links left as [LINK] placeholders.

operating_principles: Deployment-to-results: every performance result tied to a specific deployment config (this config = this performance = this cost)
Unresolved gaps carry forward every week without exception, flagged until nauseating
Track timelines by owner, commitment, and outcome — not by who is nominally in charge

portfolio_risks.risk: Eric's data pipeline plus his post-6/22 bandwidth is the shared dependency across Companion Validation, QC Tool data pull, and Maintenance monitoring/alerting.
portfolio_risks.impact: Single biggest portfolio risk — manage Eric's queue deliberately.
